Message from Chancellor
With over three decades of pioneering excellence in IT training, our evolution into formal technical education began with the establishment of a premier engineering institution VVIT offering B.Tech programs. Through unwavering commitment to academic quality and the enduring trust of our students and parents, we stand today proudly as  (VVIT University) — a State Private University poised for global impact.
At VVIT University, we believe that education is not just about imparting knowledge, but about shaping character and building futures. Our foundational philosophy — “Everyone Counts” — ensures that every student, parent, faculty member, and partner is at the heart of our journey. Our steadfast adherence to “Absolute Discipline and Absolute Education” defines the culture that drives our pursuit of excellence.
Inspired by the words of Robert Frost — “Miles to go before I sleep…” — we view this milestone not as a culmination, but as a renewed beginning. A beginning filled with responsibility, determination, and a deep sense of purpose.
I extend my heartfelt welcome to all aspirants of higher education to join VVIT University — where dreams take , and aspirations find their wings.
Campus Life at @ VVITU
At VVIT University (VVITU), we believe that education extends beyond classrooms. Our campus life is designed to empower students holistically—academically, technically, culturally, and physically. From coding labs to cultural clubs, and from yoga to inter-college tournaments, every experience at VVITU is a step toward transformation and excellence.

Our strong industry-academia partnerships play a pivotal role in curriculum enhancement, research innovation, student training, and placement opportunities.

title: Pro_Chancellor
text:
Pro-Chancellor
Sri. Vasireddy Mahadev
Pro Chancellor VVIT University
Vice Chairman - VIVA The School
Founder & CEO - Freela
Sri Mahadev Vasireddy, Vice Chairman of Vasireddy Venkatadri Institute of Technology (VVIT) and VIVA The School, is a forward-thinking leader with a strong commitment to shaping the future of education. A graduate in Computer Science from Penn State University, USA, he brings a global perspective and a dynamic approach to academic administration and institutional development. As the Chief Relations Officer of both VVIT and VIVA, he plays a key role in building strategic partnerships, fostering academic-industry collaborations, and driving innovation across campuses. The elder son of Sri Vasireddy Vidyasagar, Managing Trustee of Social Educational Trust and Vice Chairman of both institutions, Sri Mahadev Vasireddy has taken on the mantle of responsibility with passion and purpose. Known for his innovative thinking and out-of-the-box ideas, he is committed to leading VVIT and VIVA into the Industry 4.0 era. Whether it's by enhancing the digital learning environment or integrating global best practices, he constantly seeks new ways to future-proof the educational ecosystem. His ability to connect with people, especially students, and his natural leadership style make him a beloved and influential presence on campus. In recent years, Sri Mahadev Vasireddy has been instrumental in strengthening the institution’s core team and building new areas of expertise. He has played a vital role in realizing his father’s vision by transforming Vasireddy Venkatadri Institute of Technology into . His efforts have significantly contributed to elevating the academic and technical infrastructure of the institutions. His rapport with students is exceptional. As a mentor and guide to the Student Activity Council, he channels their energy and creativity, ensuring vibrant and impactful student-led initiatives. His approachable demeanor and modern outlook make him an ideal bridge between tradition and innovation. Carrying forward his father's legacy of dreaming big, Sri Mahadev Vasireddy is also leading a global tech initiative as Founder & CEO of 'Freela', a blockchain-based decentralized freelancing platform aimed at truly democratizing the gig economy. Through this venture, he aims to bring the world closer by empowering individuals with equal opportunity and autonomy. Sri Mahadev Vasireddy continues to inspire with his futuristic vision, entrepreneurial spirit, and unwavering commitment to educational excellence.

title: Vice Chancellor
text:
Vice-Chancellor
Vice-Chancellor VVIT University
Prof. Rambabu Kodali obtained his BE and M.Tech in Mechanical Engineering from MANIT Bhopal in 1980 and 1984, respectively, and earned his Ph.D. from IIT Kharagpur in 1991, with a doctoral thesis titled “Knowledge-based System for Real-time Control of Flexible Manufacturing Systems (FMS).” After submitting his Ph.D. thesis in August 1990, he joined the Department of Mechanical Engineering at IIT Delhi as a Lecturer on 30 August 1990. In 1992, he joined BITS Pilani as an Assistant Professor, was promoted to Associate Professor and Professor in 1995 and 2000, respectively, and was later nominated as the Shri S. K. Birla Chair Professor at the Pilani Campus in April 2012. At BITS Pilani, he served as Group Leader (HOD) of Mechanical Engineering from 1994 to 2010 and Group Leader (HOD) of Engineering Technology from 2004 to 2010, making significant contributions to research and academic leadership in areas such as Flexible Manufacturing Systems, Computer Integrated Manufacturing, World-class Manufacturing, Manufacturing Excellence, and Innovative Product Design. Under his leadership, the Mechanical Engineering Group achieved DST-FIST support (2002–2007) for developing a world-class manufacturing laboratory and UGC-SAP support (2007–2012) for the thrust area of innovative product design and development; he also established a state-of-the-art FMS laboratory at the Pilani Campus. Prof. Kodali initiated and developed several on-campus academic programs at BITS Pilani, including integrated first-degree programs in BE (Hons) Manufacturing Engineering (with and without Industrial Practice) and higher-degree programs in ME Design Engineering and ME Manufacturing Systems Engineering, and contributed to curricula for off-campus, work-integrated, and collaborative programs. His teaching and research interests include the Toyota Production System, Lean Manufacturing, World-class Manufacturing, Manufacturing Excellence, FMS, Manufacturing Management, and Manufacturing Systems Engineering; he has supervised 13 Ph.D. scholars and published over 200 papers in reputed journals and conference proceedings. In 2012, he became the Director of NIT Jamshedpur, serving a successful five-year tenure (2012–2017) during which he also acted as the first Mentor Director of IIIT Ranchi (2016–2017), initiating its academic processes at NIT Jamshedpur. He developed Vision 2022 and a comprehensive roadmap for NIT Jamshedpur, implementing best practices in governance, academic innovation, student and faculty welfare, industry linkages, community engagement, and infrastructure development, including MoUs with CPWD, and effectively implemented Kakodkar Committee recommendations to enhance the quality of technical education—transforming the institute “from good to great.” Subsequently, he served as Pro-Vice Chancellor of KIIT Deemed to be University, Bhubaneswar (2017–2019), and later as Director of MITS Madanapalle (Autonomous) (2019–2020), continuing his distinguished contributions to higher technical education and institutional leadership.

He, along with the Chairman, plays a pivotal role in defining the college’s vision and strategic direction, bringing invaluable experience in managing people at various levels while consistently ensuring smooth and harmonious relationships across the institution. He has extensive experience in administering and maintaining the campus, as well as in designing and executing student activities aimed at fostering the all-round development of students. His deep-rooted academic interest has been the driving force behind his enduring commitment to the field of education. Academically, he is a postgraduate from Vikram University, Ujjain, Madhya Pradesh, where he earned his Master’s degree in Geology, followed by an M.Sc. (Tech) in Mineral Exploration and an M.Phil. from the same university. A young and energetic member of the management team, he is actively involved in the day-to-day administration and strategic management of the educational institution. With his full-time dedication to management, complemented by the guidance of learned professors, the college has achieved significant milestones in technical education. He strongly believes that the nation requires not only engineers and managers but also virtuous individuals—professionals and entrepreneurs equipped with integrity, discipline, and a strong sense of citizenship—capable of contributing to the nation’s growth as a global power. Prior to his role in education management, he gained considerable experience as an Exploration Geologist with the Mineral Exploration Corporation, serving across Rajasthan, Bihar (now Jharkhand), Madhya Pradesh, and Maharashtra. He later transitioned into the education and technology sector by establishing an Aptech Computer Education unit in Eluru, which continues to operate successfully under the name Sri Sai Software and Informatics. He also served as a Project Coordinator for large-scale school computerization initiatives, including the 1000 Schools Computerization Project, ICT@5000, and ICT@1300 projects across multiple districts. Presently, he oversees the day-to-day operations of Vasireddy Venkatadri Institute of Technology, and beyond his professional commitments, he is actively engaged in agriculture in Sekuru and Chiluvuru villages, cultivating lemon and sapota orchards, paddy fields, and various seasonal crops.

title: Jt Secretary
text:
Director-Corporate Relations & Skill Development
Sri. M. Sree Krishna
Director - Corporate Relations & Skill Development
Sri Mamillapalli Sree Krishna, Joint Secretary of VVIT, holds a Master’s degree in Computer Science and Engineering from JNTUK, Kakinada, and is one of the earliest educators to begin teaching Computer Science and Engineering subjects across coastal Andhra Pradesh. After completing his engineering graduation from GITAM, Visakhapatnam, in 1987, he underwent advanced training in computer science at NIIT, where he subsequently served as a faculty member from 1989 to 1994. In 1994, he joined hands with Sri Vasireddy Vidya Sagar, a fellow young technocrat, to establish the first APTECH Computers franchise in Andhra Pradesh at Guntur, playing a key role in developing it into a prominent center of learning for aspiring software professionals. In 2007, he joined VVIT as the Joint Secretary, bringing with him deep academic and administrative expertise. An alumnus of Sainik School, Korukonda, Sri Sree Krishna is known for his astute administrative skills and has been a guiding force behind the success of the Training and Placement Cell of the college. Despite being deeply engaged in the institution’s administrative responsibilities, he consistently keeps himself updated with the latest developments in technology, reflecting his enduring passion for the field.

title: Registrar
text:
Registrar
Dr. Y. Mallikarjuna Reddy
Registrar
Dr. Yennapusa Mallikarjuna Reddy, born on 1st June 1965, is a distinguished academician and administrator with over 31 years of professional teaching and research experience. He obtained his Bachelor of Engineering in Electronics and Communication Engineering from Osmania University, Hyderabad in 1987, followed by a Master’s degree in ECE (Instrumentation and Control Systems) from JNTU Kakinada in 1990, and later earned his Ph.D. in Radar Signal and Image Processing from Osmania University, Hyderabad in March 2009. An active and prolific researcher, Dr. Mallikarjuna Reddy’s areas of specialization include Digital Signal Processing, Radar and Image Processing, Speech Processing, Missile Technology, Electromagnetic Fields, Fiber Optics, Neural Networks, Digital Communications, Machine Learning, Deep Learning, and the Internet of Things (IoT). He has published 30 research papers in peer-reviewed international and national journals, including SCI-indexed journals, as well as international conferences. As a Principal Investigator, he secured a ₹60 lakhs grant for major research projects funded by DST, and he serves as an active reviewer for reputed international journals such as AMSE Periodicals (France) – Modeling, Measurement & Control, IACSIT, and ICSCI. During his tenure as Principal, he implemented several best practices, including the Performance-Based Appraisal System (PBAS), establishment of industry-collaborative laboratories, creation of departmental incubation centers, organization of model exhibitions on Innovation Day (Engineers’ Day), promotion of research and development through faculty upskilling and publications, external academic audits, and the implementation of industry-relevant flexible curricula aligned with outcome-based education. His leadership played a key role in achieving NAAC Grade ‘A’, NBA accreditation for all B.Tech programs, and significant improvements in campus placements. Dr. Mallikarjuna Reddy was ratified as Principal of VVIT by JNTU Kakinada in 2011 and continues to perform diverse responsibilities including administration, teaching, examinations, R&D projects, campus placements, and soft skills training. He previously served as Head of the ECE Department, overseeing UG and PG programs, assisting in institutional administration, curriculum design, university examination processes, student association activities, training programs, industry–institute interaction, campus placements, soft skills development, and alumni relations. He is a Fellow of the Institution of Engineers (FIE), a Life Member of ISTE, and a Member of IEEE. In recognition of his academic excellence, he received Best Teacher Awards in 2019, 2010, 2007, 2005, and 2004 across various institutions. He is a recognized Ph.D. supervisor under JNTU Kakinada, JNTU Hyderabad, and Rayalaseema University, has authored nine textbooks in subjects such as Probability Theory and Stochastic Processes, Signals and Stochastic Processes, and Electromagnetic Waves and Transmission Lines, and is currently guiding doctoral scholars in emerging areas including Medical Image Processing, Machine Learning, and Deep Learning.

VVIT University offers a diverse range of undergraduate, postgraduate, and Ph.D. programs through its specialized schools and departments, each committed to academic excellence and industry relevance. The Department of Computer Science and Engineering, the Department of Artificial Intelligence and Data Science and the Department of Artifical Intelligence and Machine Learning prepare students for the evolving digital world with cutting-edge curricula. The Department of Electronics and Communication Engineering and the Department of Electrical & Electronics Engineering focus on foundational and advanced technologies in communication and power systems. The Department of Mechanical Engineering and the Department of Civil Engineering equip students with practical and theoretical knowledge to address real-world engineering challenges. The APPA School of Business shapes future business leaders through strong foundations in management, innovation, and entrepreneurship. In addition to undergraduate programs, VVITU offers advanced postgraduate courses and Ph.D. programs that empower students to deepen their expertise and contribute to research and innovation in their respective fields. Together, these programs provide a comprehensive academic platform for students to explore, excel, and lead in their chosen careers.

B.TECH - COMPUTER SCIENCE AND ENGINEERING (ARTIFICIAL INTELLIGENCE AND MACHINE LEARNING)
The Department of Computer Science and Engineering (CSE) at VVITU has been at the forefront of technical education since its inception in 2007. Initially we are affiliated to JNTU Kakinada. Later the department became autonomous in 2019. Now we are transformed into university. With a strong commitment to academic excellence, innovation, and industry collaboration, the department has grown significantly over the years, shaping the future of aspiring engineers.
The Department of Computer Science and Engineering at VVITU University has been at the forefront of delivering world-class education and research. With a dynamic curriculum, strong industry interface, and experienced faculty, we aim to shape tomorrow’s technology leaders. Our B. Tech (CSE) program is NBA accredited under Tier-I until June 2026, reinforcing our commitment to quality education and global academic standards.
The department boasts a team of highly qualified faculty members with expertise across various domains of computer science. Many are active researchers with publications in reputed international journals and conferences. The department is associated with multiple professional bodies like ACM, ACM-W and EWB (IUCEE) to enhance the culture of learning activities. The department focuses on annual technical symposiums, hackathons, and coding competitions. The department has consistent placement record with graduates employed in top companies such as Google, Microsoft, Amazon, Infosys, TCS, Wipro, and startups. Alumni have also excelled in higher studies at premier institutions in India and abroad.
Career Opportunities
Apart from the regular job offers for the computer science and engineering graduates, the list of job profiles for a graduate in this program would be
Data Engineer / Scientist
Machine Learning Engineer
Interactive Visualizer / Graphic Designer
Big Data Engineer / Architect
ML Intelligent System Researcher
VVITU continues to grow as a beacon of innovation and excellence, striving to empower the next generation of engineers, technologists, and leaders who are capable of making meaningful contributions to society, both nationally and globally.

This program provides in-depth expertise in the design, analysis, and optimization of mechanical components and systems. tpccoordinator@vvit.net
Placement Training Program
VVITU’s Placement Training Program is a comprehensive initiative aimed at enhancing students' employability and career readiness. The program encompasses a broad spectrum of skills development, including:
Aptitude and technical skill enhancement
Soft skill development and personality refinement
Resume crafting and optimization
Mock interviews and feedback sessions
Strategic networking opportunities with industry professionals
This structured program is designed to equip students with the requisite skills, knowledge, and competencies to excel in the competitive job market.
Industry Immersion & Exposure
VVITU provides students with immersive industry experiences through strategic partnerships with top-tier organizations. Key initiatives include:
Internships and co-op placements with leading companies, enabling hands-on experience and skill application
Hackathons, coding challenges, and project-based competitions that simulate real-world industry scenarios
Industry-academia collaborations that foster innovation, entrepreneurship, and talent development
These opportunities empower students to gain practical insights, build industry connections, and demonstrate their capabilities to potential employers.
IndustrialCollaboration
At VVIT University (VVITU), we believe in bridging the gap between academia and industry to empower students with real-world skills and future-ready capabilities. Our strong industry-academia partnerships play a pivotal role in curriculum enhancement, research innovation, student training, and placement opportunities.
VVITU continues to grow as a beacon of innovation and excellence, striving to empower the next generation of engineers, technologists, and leaders who are capable of making meaningful contributions to society, both nationally and globally.

title: Hostels
text:
•VVITAT-2026 to be held on 22-03-2026 (Sunday) & 28-03-2026 (Saturday); Morning: 09:30 AM–12:00 PM; Afternoon: 01:30 PM–04:00 PM
•VVITAT-2026 to be held on 22-03-2026 (Sunday) & 28-03-2026 (Saturday); Morning: 09:30 AM–12:00 PM; Afternoon: 01:30 PM–04:00 PM
VVIT University provides comfortable and secure hostel accommodation to create a home-away-from-home experience for students.
Three dedicated hostel blocks for girls namely Tulasi Block, Aruna Block and Sarala Block (with 1000 capacity)
One spacious hostel for boys (with 1000 capacity)
Our hostels are designed to support academic focus and healthy living, offering a blend of comfort, safety and modern amenities.
Free Wi-Fi to support uninterrupted learning and connectivity
Air-conditioned rooms
24/7 hot water supply for student convenience
Purified drinking water to ensure health and hygiene
Nutritious and hygienic food served in well-maintained dining halls
Laundry facility to meet daily student needs with ease
Digital library access within the hostel premises for extended academic resources
In-house wardens and Resident Caretakers
24/7 security with trained personnel
On-campus first-aid facility with ambulance service to handle emergencies and ensure health support. MoU with Rajarajeswari Multispecialty Hospital to take care of any ailments
Hostel Fee Calculator
Note: For fresh registration an amount of Rs.3000 is charged.
Ensure you submit complete and valid documents to avoid delays in the allocation process.
Freshman Information
Hostel Wardens are responsible for maintaining discipline, safety, and smooth day-to-day operations in the hostel.
Dr. K. Lakshma Reddy

Campus Life - The Student Activity Council
The Student Activity Council
The Student Activity Council (SAC) is the official student representative body at VVITU. It acts as a bridge between students, faculty, administration, and management—advocating for student concerns, welfare, discipline, and infrastructure-related issues.
SAC also manages club activities, co‑curricular and extra‑curricular events, and processes student feedback and initiatives across academics, hostels, transportation, and sports.
SAC Structure & Governance
SAC is organized in a three-tier hierarchical structure:
Central Working Committee (CWC)
Central Working Committee (CWC)
Core governing body, responsible for planning, executing, and overseeing SAC’s functionality.
Includes positions such as President, Vice‑President, General Secretary, and other members nominated or selected by an advisory committee.
Roles include policy framing, budget planning, club oversight, external relations, and ensuring alignment with SAC’s vision and mission.
CWC reports to the Principal and is evaluated by a Selection Commission.
President: Heads the CWC, official SAC representative, leads meetings, oversees implementation, and reports to the advisory committee. Can be removed only by Principal’s approval.
Vice‑President: Supports committees, chairs meetings in President’s absence, and proposes new structures. Removal requires Principal & advisory approval.
General Secretary: Manages documentation, agendas, budgets, club coordination, and communication across the council.
Comprises office bearers or executive heads of various clubs, committees, and projects. They carry out the policies and events designed by the CWC.
Includes all students of VVITU—by default. They are the primary beneficiaries of SAC’s activities and can provide feedback, suggestions, or grievances.
SAC Core Values & Offerings
Students as central focus
Honesty, integrity, ethics, professionalism
Diversity, inclusiveness, respect
Competitive spirit and pushing each other to excel
Feedback-driven improvement of service levels
Platforms for leadership, social, and interpersonal skill development
Spaces to initiate student-led ideas and programs
An engaged staff that listens, supports, and empathizes
A supportive, challenging environment for intellectual and practical growth
Transferable skills for future careers
To serve as a central point of student interaction inside/outside classroom
To remain aligned with VVITU’s mission and vision
To promote collaboration across departments with teamwork, open communication, and shared goals
To deepen understanding of cultural, social, and democratic responsibilities
To stay humble, reflective and focused on reaching goals and living SAC’s mission/vision fully.

text:

The National Cadet Corps is the Indian military cadet corps with its Headquarters at New Delhi. It is open to school and college students on voluntary basis. National Cadet Corps is a Tri-Services Organization, comprising the Army, Navy and Air Force, engaged in grooming the youth of the country into disciplined and patriotic citizens. The National Cadet Corps in India is a voluntary organization which recruits cadets from high schools, colleges and Universities all over India. The Cadets are given basic military training in small arms and parades. The officers and cadets have no liability for active military service once they complete their course but are given preference over normal candidates during selections based on the achievements in the corps.
NCC was firstly started in 1666 in Germany. The NCC in India was formed with the National Cadet Corps Act of 1948. It was raised on 15 July 1948. The origin of NCC can be traced back to the ‘University Corps’, which was created under the Indian Defence Act 1917, with the objective to make up the shortage of the Army. In 1920, when the Indian Territorial Act was passed, the ‘University Corps’ was replaced by the University Training Corps (UTC). The aim was to raise the status of the UTC and make it more attractive to the youth. The UTC Officers and cadets dressed like the army. It was a significant step towards the Indianisation of armed forces. It was rechristened in the form of UOTC so the National Cadet Corps can be considered as a successor of the University Officers Training Corps (UOTC) which was established by the British Government in 1942. During World War II, the UOTC never came up to the expectations set by the British. This led to the idea that some better schemes should be formed, which could train more young men in a better way, even during peace. A committee headed by Pandit H.N. Kunzru recommended a cadet organization to be established in schools and colleges at a national level. The National Cadet Corps Act was accepted by the Governor General and on 15 July 1948 the National Cadet Corps came into existence.
In 1949, the Girls Division was raised in order to give equal opportunities to school and college going girls. The NCC was given an inter-service image in 1950 when the Air Wing was added, followed by the Naval Wing in 1952. Same year, the NCC curriculum was extended to include community development/social service activities as a part of the NCC syllabus at the behest of Late Pandit Jawaharlal Nehru who took keen interest in the growth of the NCC. Following the 1962 Sino-Indian War, to meet the requirement of the Nation, the NCC training was made compulsory in 1963. In 1968, the Corps was again made voluntary.[3]
During Indo-Pakistani war of 1965 & Bangladesh-Pakistani war of 1971, NCC cadets were second line of defense. They organized camp to assist ordnance factories, supplying arms and ammunition to the front and also were used as patrol parties to capture enemy paratroopers. The NCC cadets also worked hand in hand with the Civil defence authorities and actively took part in rescue works and traffic control.
After 1965 and 1971 wars NCC syllabus was revised. Rather than just being second line of defence, NCC syllabus laid greater stress on developing quality of leadership and officer-like qualities. The military training which the NCC cadets received was reduced and greater importance was given to other areas like social service and youth management.
At Headquarters level, the NCC is headed by a Director General with the rank of lieutenant-general. He is assisted by two Additional Director Generals (A and B) of two-star rank (major-general, rear-admiral or air vice-marshal). Five Brigadier level officers and other civil officials also assist him. The Headquarters is located in Delhi. There are 17 Directorates located in the state capitals headed by an officer of the rank of a Brigadier from the three Services. Depending upon the size of the state and growth of NCC in the states, Directorates have up to 14 Group Headquarters under them through which they exercise their command and control of the organisation in the state. Each group is headed by an officer of the rank of Colonel or equivalent known as Group Commander. Each NCC Group Headquarters control 5-7 units (Bns) commanded by Lt. Colonel/Major or equivalent. Each Battalion consists of companies which are commanded by the Associate NCC Officer (ANO) of the rank of lieutenant to major. In all there are 95 Group Headquarters in the country who exercise control over a network of 667 Army Wing Units (including technical and girls unit), 60 Naval Wing Units and 61 Air Squadrons. There are two training establishments namely Officers Training School, Kamptee (Nagpur, Maharashtra) and Women Officers Training School, Gwalior.

About Institution's Innovation Council (IIC)
In the year 2018, the Ministry of Education (MoE), Government of India, through the Ministry of Education’s Innovation Cell (MIC), launched the Institution’s Innovation Council (IIC) program in collaboration with the All India Council for Technical Education (AICTE). The primary objective of the IIC initiative is to systematically foster a culture of innovation and entrepreneurship within Higher Educational Institutions (HEIs).
The Institution’s Innovation Council aims to actively engage faculty members, students, and staff in a wide range of innovation and entrepreneurship-related activities. These include ideation, problem solving, design thinking, proof of concept (PoC) development, intellectual property rights (IPR) awareness, project handling and management, and activities at the pre-incubation and incubation stages. Through these structured efforts, IICs help establish and strengthen a sustainable innovation and start-up ecosystem within academic institutions.
The IIC framework is designed to address key challenges commonly observed in HEIs, such as the limited number of innovation activities, lack of continuity and planning, minimal involvement of institutional leadership, insufficient coordination in resource mobilization and deployment, and underutilization of the creative potential of students. By overcoming these challenges, IICs play a vital role in nurturing a vibrant and cohesive innovation ecosystem.
The IIC model is unique as it integrates a flexible calendar of activities, a structured scoring and reward system, decentralized operations with clearly defined roles, continuous progress monitoring, and incentive mechanisms, all coordinated through a robust digital platform. Over the past few years, the IIC initiative has emerged as a scalable and sustainable model for promoting innovation in HEIs, contributing significantly to India’s improved performance in the Global Innovation Index (GII).
-Creation of a vibrant local innovation ecosystem within the institution
-Establishment of strong start-up support mechanisms in HEIs
-Preparation of the institute for the Atal Ranking of Institutions on Innovation Achievements (ARIIA)
-Development of a functional ecosystem for scouting innovative ideas and supporting pre-incubation activities
-Enhancement of cognitive, creative, and problem-solving abilities among students
-Conduct innovation, IPR, and entrepreneurship-related activities as prescribed by MIC in a time-bound manner
-Identify, recognize, and reward innovative ideas and achievements, and disseminate success stories
-Organize workshops, seminars, expert talks, and interactions with entrepreneurs, investors, and industry professionals, and build a strong mentor pool for student innovators
-Establish networking and collaborations with peer institutions and national entrepreneurship development organizations
-Develop and maintain an Institutional Innovation Portal to showcase innovative projects undertaken by faculty and students
-Organize hackathons, idea competitions, and innovation challenges with active participation from industry partners
